title: Irish Regulator Opens Inquiry Into Vantara AI Over Data Transfers
body: Ireland's Data Protection Commission has opened a formal inquiry into Vantara AI, the Stockholm-based machine learning startup, over its handling of personal data transferred from European users to servers in the United States. The DPC confirmed Monday that it had issued an initial questionnaire to the company after receiving complaints from privacy advocacy group DataWatch EU. A spokesperson for the commission declined to characterize the inquiry as anything more than preliminary. Vantara, which provides AI-driven customer analytics to retailers across the continent, said in a statement that it "fully cooperates with all relevant regulators" and believes its data practices are compliant with GDPR. The company's CEO, Nils Bergström, was not available for comment. The inquiry does not constitute a finding of wrongdoing. Similar investigations by the DPC into U.S. tech firms have in the past taken years to conclude and have resulted in fines ranging from nominal sums to record-breaking penalties. DataWatch EU claimed the company's standard contractual clauses are "boilerplate and legally deficient," a characterization Vantara rejected.
